title: HeadObject, GetObject, Ranges, and SdkStream Cleanup · Type Forensics
summary: A Node incident packet contains a plausible bounded TypeScript download flow with one unsafe assertion, stale object identity, unbounded stream, lost rejection, abort leak, cache defect, policy gap, or recovery overclaim; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded TypeScript download flow and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose HEAD, GET, conditional GET, or a metadata database lookup without doubling requests or trusting stale authority.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, check response and metadata, own and close the body, propagate cancellation, handle partial reads, and distinguish transport from caller writes.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, map application validators and preconditions to S3 requests and preserve 304, 412, not-found, and version-specific meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Head Vs Get
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 4266 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate a versioned conditional range and prove SdkStream cleanup; keep AbortSignal and stream cleanup under one named owner; then abort during a stream or multipart promise and prove every task and part reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose HEAD, GET, conditional GET, or a metadata database lookup without doubling requests or trusting stale authority.

Inspect the Head Vs Get trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Head Vs Get.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, choose head, get, conditional get, or a metadata database lookup without doubling requests or trusting stale authority. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Head Vs Get, keep Head Vs Get connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Download Error Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-download-error-boundary-17dtys" that uses case 2409 to validate a versioned conditional range and prove SdkStream cleanup. Incident diagnosis: preserve the symptom, isolate first cause, and prove a regression case before repair. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
